reviews.review_msg: Take Math 100 instead
Honestly ok course I had previous knowledge so it was easy to answer the questions and tests however Dr. Peter was helpful with something's but not all as for computing assignments.
dont take him

The course lacked structure and clear communication from start to finish. Concepts were not explained thoroughly, and feedback was minimal, making it difficult to understand how to improve. The stress came from confusion rather than the material itself. Overall, I cannot recommend this professor.

Despite putting in significant effort, the lack of organization and transparency made success feel unnecessarily difficult. Instructions were often unclear, and support was limited. This experience was disappointing and discouraging.
Many quizzes and doesn't really explain

Instructor assumes students already know concepts, offers minimal guidance, explanations are rushed. I struggled to understand foundational material because I didn't get step-by-step help which wasted a lot of time as I had to go back and teach it myself!

He's a nice guy, but a bad teacher. His teaching style really confused me and seemed to stretch unnecessary things super long. He didn't update deadlines on time, and gave multiple the same day. He never returned tests on time, and I was really stressed in the group project.

LISTEN TO ME DONT TAKE ANYTHING WITH HIM!!!
I honestly regret enrolling in this class, the problem isn't with the subject its with the professor, I have never worked this hard to teach myself material I paid to be taught, I would not take another course with this professor under any circumstance, By far the worst educational experience I've had.

He doesn't teach, like at all, there's no clear explanation on anything and you have to figure out everything yourself, which isn't great for someone who already struggles with mathematics. Try to avoid if you're not interested in sleepless nights.
had to do everything myself

He cares about his student's learning as much as he cares about air resistance. 
His ego is held together by Van der Waals force.

Participation matters in both 140/141 be sure to do the pre lectures, read the material, and ask questions after you have read/understand the concepts. Prof is not bad, at first it might be intimidating but he's there to help you. Midterms are based on problem solving / expertTA and the experiments
You'll do well if you do his problem solvings, experTA and samples. He helps in office hours.
Yeah just avoid him
Great guy and good father, awful teacher.
hes a great prof and a great guy
If  ur an international student,do not take his course,and you should learn all the things by youself before tests,his class offer 0 help,and moody,that's the best word to describe peter.
Just another narcissist
He gets very moody and very demanding. One of the worst courses/professor pairings I have ever had.
Yes he is funny but it wont make course any easy with him. Just do yourself favor and avoid him.
Wish he was more passionate in teaching, not half baked interest and attitude towards students in physics
I'll make it 40 (1 star) reviews with this one. He is very moody and the best father in the world, which also makes him take his job as a joke.
Definitely my worst nightmare going taking his class

I'd say he's a funny person, great dad. But as a professor the method he teach might not be for everyone, you HAVE TO READ and UNDERSTAND the textbook and that is. Don't rely on what he teach in class it way different in the exam. You have to pass all the midterms, do all the class assignment and homework and finish his final project.
BEST PROFESSOR AT FIC. DON'T BELIEVE THE OTHER REVIEWS. HE IS AN AMAZING PERSON.
He is a kind and hilarious teacher. I like his teaching method as well. Don't be cheated by the mark he got here!

Even though this is a studio class so its expected that you should know the basics for some of the topics, the problem is that the textbook he instructs us to read is a level above our current knowledge. The questions on ExpertTA is based on the content in the textbook. Some of the questions are impossible to solve unless you took MATH152

I really like him but I'd be lying if I were to say my 3 mental breakdowns arent because of this class.
If you are a student in his class, prepare to: study independently of the classes, utilise peer education, and help each other.
An usually lecturers from 10 to 1, he sort of breaks it from 10 to 7 - 5.
Studying the quizzes is a good method too.
Many people complain about Peter but I sincerely believe that in general they have not seen that physics is always a intensive course and requires discipline. Not everything is rosy.
